_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot, federal AK+AL batch)
_meta.note: Federal evidence-finalize batch (5): AK-AL Rep Begich (R, freshman 119th), former AK Rep Peltola (now D 2026 US SENATE candidate vs Sullivan — CURRENCY FIX), AL-2 Rep Figures (D freshman 119th — 3 cross-party immigration/fentanyl YES votes), AL-4 Rep Aderholt (R senior appropriator, Pro-Life Caucus co-chair — cross-party hawk on Ukraine/Israel supplemental + AGAINST Yemen War Powers), AL-5 Rep Strong (R freshman 118th, Mt. Zion Baptist, defense-district captive — Trump-endorsed unopposed in 2026 R primary). Mike Rogers (AL-3 R) DROPPED from batch for slug-collision with MD-32 Delegate Mike Rogers (D).

profile.religion: Protestant — self-identifies 'born-again Christian' (Alaska Watchman Q&A 2024-06-12): 'I was raised in the church... I am a born-again Christian.' Specific church not publicly stated.
profile.background: U.S. Representative AK-At-Large (R), sworn Jan 3, 2025 (119th Congress); defeated incumbent Mary Peltola 51.3-48.7 in 2024 RCV runoff. Baylor BBA + Indiana U Bloomington MBA. Founder, FarShore Partners (software). Grandson of Rep. Nick Begich Sr. (D, lost in 1972 plane crash); nephew of former Sen. Mark Begich (D) — the only Republican in a leading Alaska Democratic dynasty. Married Dharna Vakharia (2002); one son. Lives Chugiak, AK. Committees: Natural Resources (Vice Chair, Energy & Mineral Resources sub), Transportation & Infrastructure (Vice Chair, Railroads/Pipelines sub), Science Space & Technology. Caucuses: Republican Study Committee, Congressional Western Caucus (EVC). Not in House Freedom Caucus.

nick-begich-iii.notes_set: U.S. Rep AK-AL (R), freshman 119th Congress (sworn Jan 3, 2025). [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action 119th lifetime 92% — orthodox RSC/conservative House line. Hard-evidence wins: original co-sponsor + YES on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Jan 23 2025, 217-204); YES + co-sponsor of SAVE Act (proof-of-citizenship voter registration); supports physical border barriers 'on land and within waterways' + Remain in Mexico; supports Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity; '100% supportive of the 2A'; opposes counting non-citizens in apportionment; supports parental notification for minor abortions, Hyde, CPC funding; endorsed Trump transgender military ban; opposes Biden Mifepristone pharmacy distribution. PRO-LIFE POSTURE: state-deference framework (Dobbs returns to states), NOT abolitionist — endorses restrictions (Hyde, parental notification, CPC funding) but doesn't advocate constitutional abolition. EMILYs List Women Vote spent against him in 2024. No material cross-party deviations from RSC line.

0.note: Pro-life via Dobbs/states framework; no on-record affirmation of personhood-from-conception.

1.note: Endorses restrictions (Hyde, parental notification, CPC funding); explicitly defers to states — not documented as abolition advocate (nor as opponent of abolition).

4.note: EMILYs List Women Vote ran 2024 attack ads AGAINST him — confirms he never accepted abortion-industry PAC funding.

2.note: Endorsed Trump transgender military ban; Heritage 92% includes pro-biological-sex women's sports / EO restoration votes.

4.note: Explicitly opposes DEI/ESG capture in military — opposes LGBTQ-ideology displacement of readiness mission.

1.note: Supports parental notification for minor abortions; broader parents-rights framing.

2.note: 'Nuclear family is under attack by those who believe it is the State... that should raise the next generation'; defends family values in public schools; Heritage 92% incl. anti-CRT/SOGI votes.

0.note: Publicly identifies as 'born-again Christian'; grounds policy in 'Judeo-Christian faith' as nation's founding.

4.note: Explicitly opposes ESG/DEI policy capture in DoD.

1.note: Voted YES and co-sponsored SAVE Act requiring documentary proof of citizenship at voter registration.

0.note: 'We need physical barriers on land and within waterways' — supports wall + Remain in Mexico.

4.note: Opposes counting non-citizens in congressional apportionment; called Dem inclusion vote 'outrageous.'

0.note: Supports Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act; '100% supportive of the Second Amendment.'
Opposes Biden pharmacy distribution of Mifepristone via executive overreach; defends informed-consent (reversal info) framing.

set.office: Former US Representative AK-AL · 2026 D U.S. Senate Candidate (challenging Sullivan)

profile.religion: Russian Orthodox (Orthodox Church in Alaska — sizeable AK Native demographic via 19th-c. Russian American mission heritage). Yup'ik name: Akalleq.
profile.background: Former U.S. Rep AK-At-Large (D), Sep 13 2022 – Jan 3 2025. Won 2022 special election (Aug 16 2022) over Sarah Palin via RCV — first Alaska Native ever elected to Congress, first woman to AK's at-large House seat. Lost 2024 re-election to Nick Begich III. ON JAN 12 2026 announced 2026 U.S. Senate campaign challenging incumbent Sen. Dan Sullivan (R). Former ED Kuskokwim River Inter-Tribal Fish Commission; AK state House 1999–2009 (Bethel). Russian Orthodox / Yup'ik. Father Ward Sattler (German-American pilot/teacher); mother Elizabeth 'LizAnn' Williams (Yup'ik). Husband Eugene 'Buzzy' Peltola Jr. died Sept 12 2023 in a Piper Super Cub crash near St. Mary's, AK; 7 children (blended).

mary-peltola.notes_set: Former US Rep AK-AL (D, 2022–2025) · NOW 2026 U.S. Senate Candidate (announced Jan 12 2026, challenging Sen. Sullivan). [2026-06 federal autopilot — currency-fix from generic '2026 candidate'] Heritage Action 118th: 18% — roughly 3-4x the typical House Dem; Alaska Public Media (2024): 'one of the least loyal Democrats in the U.S. House.' Score is based on her REAL 118th Congress voting record (one full term). HARD-LEFT on life/marriage/family/parental rights/election integrity (NO Born-Alive, YES Respect for Marriage Act, NO Parents Bill of Rights, NO HR2/Laken Riley, co-sponsor Equality Act, PP 100%). KEY CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS (high-value catches): (1) **2A — voted YES to defund Biden's Office of Gun Violence Prevention** (Nov 2023, Rosendale amdt) — one of only TWO House Dems; subsistence-hunter framing; publicly opposed complete assault-weapons ban citing food security. (2) **BORDER — voted YES on End the Border Catastrophe Act (HR 3602, May 2024)** with all Rs present; YES on bill to deport undocumented migrants who assault police. (3) **ISRAEL — voted YES on standalone Israel Security Supplemental** (Apr 20 2024, 366-58). (4) **NDAA — YES on final FY24 NDAA compromise** (Dec 2023, only 6 House Dems). NRA-PVF endorsed her over Begich in 2024 (controversial — actual NRA letter grade was 'D').

0.note: Voted NO on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (HR 26, Jan 11 2023, RC #29) — voted with all Dems except Cuellar.

1.note: SBA Pro-Life America scorecard: failing — voted to remove protections for unborn and eliminate Hyde-style funding restrictions.

2.note: Rated 100% by Planned Parenthood Action Fund; co-sponsored Women's Health Protection Act in 118th Congress.

3.note: Voted NO on House FY24 NDAA specifically citing pro-life amendments (DoD abortion-travel reimbursement block) as reason — opposed life-side restrictions.

4.note: EMILYs List Women Vote ran a major 2024 IE campaign supporting her (most-expensive cycle spend); PP 100% rating reflects abortion-industry alignment.

0.note: Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act (HR 8404, Dec 8 2022, 258-169); statement: 'major victory for freedom, privacy, dignity, and equality.'

1.note: Co-sponsor of HR 15 Equality Act (118th) — adds SOGI to Civil Rights Act with no religious-liberty carve-out.

2.note: Voted NO on House FY24 NDAA over GOP amendment limiting gender-affirming care for trans troops — opposed restrictions on trans-ideology spending.

4.note: Equality Act co-sponsor — federalizes LGBTQ promotion in employment, education, housing, public accommodations.

1.note: Voted NO on Parents Bill of Rights Act (HR 5, Mar 24 2023, 213-208); stated reason: 'students should have some right to privacy' — i.e., against parental notification on gender/social-transition.

2.note: Same NO vote on Parents Bill of Rights opposed federal parental-rights / curriculum-transparency framework.

1.note: Co-sponsor of Equality Act, which lacks RFRA carve-out — would compel Christian professionals/businesses on SOGI.

0.note: Voted NO on HR 2 Secure the Border Act (May 11 2023).

1.note: Voted NO on Laken Riley Act (HR 7511, Mar 7 2024); 37 Dems voted yes — Peltola was NOT among them.

2.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: Voted YES on End the Border Catastrophe Act (HR 3602, May 2024) and YES on bill to deport undocumented migrants who assault police officers — joined Republicans.

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: publicly opposed total Assault Weapons Ban citing subsistence hunting / food security — 'right out of the gate, no, I don't support a complete ban on all assault rifles.' (Caveat: she DOES support universal background checks, waiting periods, storage laws.)

4.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: Voted YES to defund Biden's Office of Gun Violence Prevention (Rosendale amdt, Nov 2023) — one of only 2 House Dems. Hardest 2A vote of the Congress for a Democrat.

profile.religion: Protestant (per Pew Research 'Faith on the Hill 2025' member list); specific denomination and church not publicly reported.
profile.background: U.S. Rep AL-2 (D), sworn Jan 3, 2025 (119th Congress) — first elected Nov 2024 after Allen v. Milligan redrew AL-2 into a majority-Black 'Black Belt' district. Mobile, AL native (b. Sep 3 1985). BA + JD University of Alabama. Career: Obama 2012 campaign, White House Presidential Personnel Office, then DOJ — Chief of Staff/Deputy Chief of Staff to Deputy AG (Holder/Lynch); senior counselor to AG Merrick Garland through Oct 2023. Married Kalisha 2018; 3 children. Son of slain AL state Sen. Michael Figures and current AL state Sen. Vivian Davis Figures; nephew of Thomas Figures (federal prosecutor in the Michael Donald lynching case). Committees: Transportation & Infrastructure, Agriculture (Nutrition & Foreign Agriculture sub). Caucus: Congressional Progressive Caucus (per GovTrack).

shomari-figures.notes_set: U.S. Rep AL-2 (D), freshman 119th Congress (sworn Jan 3, 2025). [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action 119th: 0% — bottom-of-rank conservative score; near-textbook Progressive Caucus Democrat. Reliably progressive on life/marriage/parental rights/2A/election integrity: NO on Born-Alive (HR 21), NO on Save Women's Sports (HR 28), NO on Parents Bill of Rights-style PROTECT Kids Act (HR 2616), NO on SAVE Act (citizenship verification), NO on Veterans 2nd Amendment Protection Act (HR 1041). KEY CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS (3-vote immigration-enforcement / fentanyl bloc): (1) **YES on Laken Riley Act** (HR 29, Jan 22 2025) — mandatory ICE detention of illegal aliens arrested for theft/violent crimes; (2) **YES on Preventing Violence Against Women by Illegal Aliens Act** (HR 30, Jan 21 2025); (3) **YES on HALT Fentanyl Act** (HR 27, Feb 6 2025) — schedules fentanyl analogues as Schedule I. Pattern: cross-votes only on immigration enforcement + fentanyl; holds Dem line elsewhere.

0.note: Voted NAY on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (HR 21, Jan 23 2025).

1.note: Heritage Action 119th: 0% — no pro-life vote alignment recorded.
Voted NO on PROTECT Kids Act (HR 2616, Roll 184, May 20 2026) — requires schools to notify parents of student's gender-identity changes.

2.note: Voted NAY on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act / Save Women's Sports (HR 28, Jan 14 2025) — opposed restrictions on biological-male participation.

0.note: Heritage Action 119th: 0% — opposes fiscal-conservative agenda across the board (broad-indicator only; no Begich-specific CBDC vote isolated).

1.note: Voted NAY on SAVE Act (HR 22, citizenship verification for voter registration, Apr 10 2025).
CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: Voted YEA on Laken Riley Act (HR 29, Jan 22 2025) — mandatory detention of illegal aliens arrested for theft/violent crimes.

2.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: Voted YEA on Preventing Violence Against Women by Illegal Aliens Act (HR 30, Jan 21 2025) AND YEA on HALT Fentanyl Act (HR 27, Feb 6 2025).

0.note: Voted NAY on Veterans 2nd Amendment Protection Act (HR 1041, Roll 190, May 21 2026).

profile.religion: Methodist (United Methodist family roots — mother Mary Frances was a lifelong member of Haleyville First UMC; father Bobby Ray was a part-time Congregational minister). Specific current congregation not publicly stated.
profile.background: U.S. Rep AL-4 (R) since Jan 1997 — 15th term begins 2025 (one of the most senior House Republicans). Birmingham-Southern College (history/poli-sci); J.D. Samford-Cumberland. Senior Appropriator: Chairman, Labor-HHS-Education Appropriations Subcommittee in the 119th Congress; also serves on Defense and Agriculture Appropriations Subcommittees. Co-Chair, House Values Action Team. Member, Congressional Pro-Life Caucus; Tea Party Caucus. Wife Caroline McDonald Aderholt; two children. Born Haleyville, AL (July 22, 1965). 2026 re-election: Trump-endorsed, qualified for ballot Jan 2026; faces token primary; AL-4 is R+33 (most-Republican House district in America).

robert-aderholt.notes_set: U.S. Rep AL-4 (R), 15th-term senior House Appropriations chairman of Labor-HHS-Ed subcommittee. [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action lifetime ~66% — markedly LOWER than Freedom Caucus peers; consistent with senior-appropriator status (manages, not slashes, the federal budget). Strong social-conservative record: NO on Respect for Marriage Act BOTH 2022 votes ('marriage is between a man and a woman' + free-exercise concerns); YES on Born-Alive + Parents Bill of Rights + Save Women's Sports + Laken Riley + SAVE Act + HR2 Secure the Border + CBDC Anti-Surveillance Act; FY26 L-HHS-Ed markup ELIMINATES Planned Parenthood funding; introduced HR 6176 Protect Funding for Women's Healthcare; SBA Pro-Life 100% (116th); National Right to Life endorsed; NRA A; FADA co-sponsor; co-sponsor National Right-to-Carry Reciprocity (HR 822). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS (high-value): (1) **HAWK on foreign-policy restraint** — Voted YES on April 2024 Ukraine + Israel + Indo-Pacific supplemental aid package (against Massie/Freedom Caucus restraint position); explicitly tied vote to AL-made Patriot/anti-drone industry ($1.5B + $500M). (2) Voted AGAINST Yemen War Powers resolution (H.J.Res.37) — opposed restricting US involvement in Saudi-led Yemen war. (3) Structural defense/pharma/agribusiness appropriator — sits on all three industry vectors (L-HHS-Ed + Defense + Agriculture Approps) with no documented Pentagon-audit or pharma-liability-repeal votes.

0.note: Co-Chair House Values Action Team; SBA 100% (116th); House Pro-Life Caucus member.

1.note: Voted YES Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act; signed discharge petition; National Right to Life lifetime endorsed.

2.note: Introduced HR 6176 Protect Funding for Women's Healthcare; FY26 L-HHS-Ed bill zeroes Planned Parenthood funding.

3.note: Maintains Hyde annually as L-HHS-Ed Chair; offered Dornan (DC Hyde) restoration; ERLC-endorsed detainee abortion-funding ban amendment.

4.note: National Right to Life endorsed; SBA 100%; no PP/NARAL/EMILYs funding.

0.note: Public statement on RFMA vote: 'marriage is between a man and a woman.'

1.note: Voted NO on Respect for Marriage Act both July 19, 2022 AND Dec 8, 2022.

2.note: Voted YES Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (HR 734, Apr 20 2023); led House Values Action Team support.

4.note: Co-sponsored First Amendment Defense Act (FADA) — protects religious objectors on marriage definition.

1.note: Voted YES Parents Bill of Rights Act (HR 5, Mar 24 2023); statement defended parental authority vs 'educational bureaucracy.'

4.note: Chair, House Values Action Team — coalition org on family-policy bills.

1.note: FADA co-sponsor protecting Christian conscience on marriage definition; cited free-exercise concerns as reason for RFMA NO.

3.note: Publicly opposed Biden federal employer/employee vaccine mandate ('totally against forced submission').

0.note: Voted YES CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (HR 5403, May 23 2024).

2.note: Supported Balanced Budget Amendment (HJR1, 2009); fiscal-restraint rhetoric — though appropriator role means he votes for omnibus packages (Heritage 66% lifetime).

1.note: Voted YES SAVE Act (HR 8281, July 10 2024) requiring citizenship proof for voter registration.

0.note: Voted YES HR 2 Secure the Border Act (May 11 2023); not among the 2 GOP defectors (Burchett/Massie).

1.note: Co-sponsored AND voted YES Laken Riley Act (HR 29 Jan 7 2025; final passage Jan 22 2025).

2.note: SAVE Act YES; press statement cited Alabama non-citizen voter-roll case — supports federal preemption against sanctuary frameworks.

0.note: Co-sponsored National Right-to-Carry Reciprocity Act (HR 822); NRA 'A' rating.

1.note: NRA A rating; consistent opposition to gun-control restrictions.

2.note: Co-sponsored Firearms Interstate Commerce Reform Act — partial NFA/GCA rollback direction.

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: Voted AGAINST H.J.Res.37 Yemen War Powers resolution — opposed restricting US involvement in Saudi-led Yemen war (failed the war-powers restraint position).

2.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: Voted YES April 2024 Ukraine + Israel + Indo-Pacific supplemental package (against Massie/Freedom Caucus restraint position); explicitly tied vote to AL-made Patriot/anti-drone industry.

profile.religion: Baptist — family attends Mt. Zion Baptist Church (the church Strong attended as a child); as Madison County Commission chair he instituted a prayer before every meeting.
profile.background: U.S. Rep AL-5 (R), sworn Jan 3, 2023 (118th Congress); 2nd term. Born Monrovia, AL. 26 years in local government; first-ever Republican chair of Madison County Commission; youngest Republican elected official in Alabama. Family bus/charter business. Wife Laura Toney Strong (RN); two children (Whitney, Harrison). Committees (119th): Appropriations (Vice Chair, Commerce/Justice/Science sub + THUD + Legislative Branch); Homeland Security (Chair, Emergency Management & Technology sub); Science, Space & Technology. AL-5 = Huntsville/Madison/Tennessee Valley — Redstone Arsenal, NASA Marshall Space Flight Center, Space Force HQ. 2026: GOP primary CANCELED, advanced unopposed; Trump-endorsed.

dale-strong.notes_set: U.S. Rep AL-5 (R), 2nd-term defense-district incumbent (Huntsville/Redstone Arsenal). [2026-06 federal autopilot] Heritage Action lifetime 77% — solid mid-tier conservative, NOT Freedom Caucus. Strong social-conservative record: YES on Born-Alive (HR 26 2023 + HR 21 2025, co-sponsored); YES Parents Bill of Rights (HR 5); YES Save Women's Sports (HR 734); YES SAVE Act (cited AL Sec of State's finding of 3,251 non-citizens on rolls); YES Laken Riley Act (attended White House signing); YES HR 2 Secure the Border; YES CBDC Anti-Surveillance Act; YES One Big Beautiful Bill (HR 1 2025 — incl. PP Medicaid defund). Lifetime NRA member. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: Voted NO on HR 8035 April 2024 Ukraine supplemental (while YES on HR 8034 Israel supplemental) — but cited Biden border failures, NOT principled war-powers restraint (populist-MAGA pattern). CAVEAT — DEFENSE-DISTRICT CAPTURE: structurally embedded in Boeing/Northrop/Lockheed/Pentagon ecosystem via Redstone earmarks ($500M+ NDAA wins); YES on $883.7B FY25 + $901B FY26 NDAA despite 7 consecutive Pentagon audit failures; only Pentagon-restraint signal is a symbolic FY24 amendment redirecting unused border-wall materials.

0.note: Voted YES on HR 26 Born-Alive (Jan 11 2023) and HR 21 Born-Alive (Jan 23 2025); co-sponsored.

1.note: Self-identifies pro-life on official site; consistent pro-life voting record per first-100-days statement.

4.note: Voted YES on HR 1 OBBBA (2025), which defunds Planned Parenthood Medicaid funding; no PP/NARAL/EMILYs contributions documented.

2.note: Voted YES on HR 734 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Apr 20 2023, party-line 219-203) — rejects trans-ideology in athletics.

1.note: Voted YES on HR 5 Parents Bill of Rights Act (Mar 24 2023).

2.note: YES on Save Women's Sports + YES on Parents Bill of Rights — rejects gender-ideology framework in K-12.

0.note: Family attends Mt. Zion Baptist; instituted prayer before every Madison County Commission meeting — public Christian witness.
Voted YES on HR 1 OBBBA (extending TCJA tax cuts and conservative fiscal agenda).

1.note: Voted YES on SAVE Act (citizenship proof for voter registration); statement cited AL Sec of State finding 3,251 non-citizens on voter rolls.

0.note: YES on HR 2 Secure the Border Act; AUTHORED FY24 NDAA amendment forcing DoD to transfer unused border-wall materials to border states.

1.note: Voted YES on Laken Riley Act; attended White House signing ceremony.

0.note: Lifetime NRA member; official Gun Rights issues page commits to opposing any restrictions on the 2A.
